challenges.title: BitShift Phantom
challenges.description: I dont know what is this ‰çáÂçªÁù•Ê±£ÊΩ≠ÊïüÁëØÂΩ¢Ê•¥ÂΩ≥Ê°©Êô¥Ê•ÆÊùü„à∞„à∂Á¥Ý can you decode it please.
challenges.hint: You can find so much decoders online.

challenges.title: NullSec Auth
challenges.description: A simple login form with no input validation. Bypass the login and access the user dashboard to get the flag. Try entering special characters in the username field and observe what happens.
challenges.hint: Try entering ' in the username field and see what happens. SQL comments can hide the password check. Using -- might help skip the password validation.

challenges.title: DevLeak Prototype
challenges.description: API keys are accidentally exposed in the frontend code. Find the API key in the JavaScript file to retrieve the flag. The flag is hidden somewhere in the client-side source code.
challenges.hint: Check the browser's Network tab or view page source. Look in JavaScript files for hardcoded strings. API keys and secrets are often left in dev code.

challenges.title: Echo Chamber
challenges.description: A comment system reflects user input without sanitization. Create a malicious URL that extracts a user's session cookie when clicked. If you can trick an admin into clicking it, you can steal their cookie which contains the flag.
challenges.hint: Try entering HTML tags in the search box and see if your input appears in the page source. JavaScript can access cookies via document.cookie.

challenges.title: DropZone CMS
challenges.description: A profile picture upload function doesn't validate file types properly. Upload a PHP shell and execute commands on the server to read the flag from flag.txt.
challenges.hint: What if you rename a .php file to .jpg? Try uploading with double extensions like shell.php.jpg. Check where uploaded files are stored.

challenges.title: ZeroTrust Portal
challenges.description: A JWT token is used but the signature verification is weak. Forge an admin token by modifying the existing user token using the "none" algorithm attack.
challenges.hint: JWT tokens have three parts separated by dots. Try decoding the middle part (it is Base64). Some servers accept "none" as a signing algorithm.

challenges.title: Puppeteer's Forge
challenges.description: A custom greeting page uses user input in a Jinja2 template engine without sanitization. Inject template syntax to explore the environment and escalate to remote code execution to read the flag file.
challenges.hint: Try entering math expressions like {{7*7}}. Template engines evaluate expressions. You might access built-in functions and modules to escalate to RCE.

challenges.title: DoubleBind Gateway
challenges.description: A fund transfer form checks the balance before processing but has a delay between the check and the deduction. Send multiple rapid requests to exceed your account balance and exploit the TOCTOU vulnerability.
challenges.hint: What if you send multiple requests at the exact same time? Try using threading or parallel requests. Look for timing issues in the payment logic.

challenges.title: Specter Payload
challenges.description: A PHP application uses unserialize() on user input. Craft a malicious serialized PHP object to exploit the __destruct magic method and execute arbitrary file reads.
challenges.hint: Look for unserialize() in the source code. Objects have magic methods like __destruct that run automatically. Try setting the file property to /etc/passwd.

challenges.title: Cascade Breach
challenges.description: An invoice system uses predictable IDs and has nested permissions. Chain multiple IDOR vulnerabilities to access another company's admin panel and download the flag.
challenges.hint: Try changing the ID numbers in URLs. Can you access resources that aren't yours? If you find one IDOR, look for more in related endpoints.

challenges.title: HexEcho Protocol
challenges.description: A compiled C binary contains a hardcoded flag string in its .data section. Use the strings command or similar tools to extract the hidden flag.
challenges.hint: Use the strings command on the binary file. Look for printable character sequences. Fake flags may be present to mislead you.

challenges.title: StackShadow
challenges.description: A vulnerable program prints the flag if you overflow the buffer with a specific pattern. Classic buffer overflow with stack variables ‚Äî the flag is stored near the overflowed buffer. Simple pattern: overflow with A characters then read from the specific offset.
challenges.hint: Input buffer is smaller than you might think

challenges.title: HexDump Autopsy
challenges.description: A file contains an encoded flag that can be decoded by analyzing its hex dump. Each character of the flag is stored as a raw hex byte. Use hexdump, xxd, or similar tools to view the hex values and decode them to ASCII.
challenges.hint: Use hexdump or xxd to view file contents

challenges.title: CipherVeil
challenges.description: A program uses simple ROT13 encoding to hide the flag. Identify the ROT13 substitution cipher and decode the obfuscated flag. The encoded string is visible in the binary ‚Äî look for the PTF{} pattern and apply ROT13 to reverse it.
challenges.hint: ROT13 shifts each letter by 13 positions. Use an online ROT13 decoder or write a simple script. Look for encoded string starting with PTF{.

challenges.title: Binary Specter
challenges.description: Reverse engineer a simple x86 binary to find a hardcoded password. The compiled C program performs a strcmp check against user input. Use objdump, GDB, or a disassembler to analyze the assembly and extract the password.
challenges.hint: Use objdump -d or gdb to disassemble. Look for string comparisons in the assembly. Find the cmp instruction that checks the password.

challenges.title: PyPhantom
challenges.description: Analyze a compiled Python (.pyc) file to extract the flag. The Python 3.x bytecode contains an obfuscated flag that is XOR-encoded. Decompile using uncompyle6 or pycdc to recover the original source code and find the flag.
challenges.hint: Use uncompyle6 to decompile .pyc files. Python bytecode can be reversed easily. Look for the XOR decoding logic in the decompiled code.

challenges.title: Nexus Obfuscator
challenges.description: Heavily obfuscated JavaScript contains the flag. Deobfuscate the JavaScript to find the flag. Use beautifiers and deobfuscators like js-beautify, JSUnpack, or Prettier. The flag might be constructed at runtime.
challenges.hint: Copy-paste JS into js-beautify.com (FREE). Look for String.fromCharCode patterns. Check for eval() statements that might build strings.

challenges.title: ShellVeil
challenges.description: Binary is packed/compressed and needs to be unpacked to analyze. Unpack and analyze the protected executable. Try UPX unpacking or identify the custom packing mechanism.
challenges.hint: Check if binary is UPX packed using strings (FREE). Try upx -d packed_binary -o unpacked_binary. Use strings on unpacked binary to find flag.

challenges.title: KeyForge Prototype
challenges.description: Binary uses encryption with a hardcoded key that must be extracted. Find cryptographic keys in the binary and decrypt the data. Look for AES/DES/RC4 key schedules, S-box initializations, or common crypto constants.
challenges.hint: Look for key schedule or S-box initialization (FREE). Search for common crypto constants. Use GDB to inspect memory for key patterns.

challenges.title: Caesar's Ghost
challenges.description: Message encrypted with Caesar cipher. Decrypt the cipher (ROT3 / shift by 3) to get the flag. Can use Python, CyberChef, or online tools. Brute-force with 25 possible shifts.
challenges.hint: Caesar cipher shifts each letter by fixed amount (FREE). Try all 25 possible shifts manually or with script (5 pts). Result should contain readable English text (5 pts).

challenges.title: Exclusive Breach
challenges.description: Message encrypted with XOR operation using a single byte key. Find the XOR key and decrypt the message. Brute-force with 256 attempts, look for readable ASCII text output.
challenges.hint: XOR each byte with all possible keys (0-255) (FREE). Look for output containing "CGS{" pattern (5 pts). High ASCII values suggest wrong key (5 pts).

challenges.title: AlphaWeave
challenges.description: Message is Base64 encoded and contains the flag. Decode the Base64 string to find the flag. Use base64 command, Python, or online tools like base64decode.org.
challenges.hint: Base64 alphabet: A-Za-z0-9+/= (FREE). Use base64 -d command to decode (5 pts). Or use Python: base64.b64decode() (5 pts).

challenges.title: Frequency Phantom
challenges.description: Simple substitution cipher where each letter maps to another (monoalphabetic). Use frequency analysis to break the cipher. English letter frequency: E, T, A, O, I, N. Use online solver at quipqiup.com.
challenges.hint: Most common letters in English: E, T, A, O (FREE). Common words: THE, AND, THAT, HAVE (5 pts). Use online solver: quipqiup.com (5 pts).

challenges.title: Prisma RSA
challenges.description: RSA encryption with weak parameters (small primes that can be factored). Factor N and decrypt the RSA message. N is small enough to factor with trial division. Use Pollard's rho for faster factorization.
challenges.hint: Factor N using trial division or Pollard's rho (FREE). Once you have p and q, compute d = modinv(e, (p-1)*(q-1)) (5 pts). Decrypt: m = pow(c, d, N) (5 pts).

challenges.title: Pattern Oracle
challenges.description: Encrypted data using AES in ECB mode (insecure). ECB encrypts identical plaintext blocks to identical ciphertext blocks, revealing patterns. Use the weakness to decrypt or identify the vulnerability.
challenges.hint: ECB encrypts same plaintext blocks identically (FREE). Identical ciphertext blocks = identical plaintext blocks (5 pts). Can perform block manipulation attacks (5 pts).

challenges.title: HashBreak Protocol
challenges.description: Hash-based authentication vulnerable to collision attacks. MD5 is cryptographically broken ‚Äî known collisions exist. Find or use a known MD5 collision to bypass hash verification.
challenges.hint: MD5 produces same hash for different inputs (collisions) (FREE). Example collision available online (5 pts). Use hashclash or fastcoll to generate collisions (5 pts).

challenges.title: Vigen√®re's Shadow
challenges.description: Vigen√®re cipher with a partially known plaintext. Use the known plaintext to recover the repeating key, then decrypt the full message to find the flag.
challenges.hint: If you know part of plaintext, recover key: key[i] = (ciphertext[i] - plaintext[i]) % 26 for each alpha position (FREE). Repeating key reveals structure (5 pts).

challenges.title: Nonce Exposure
challenges.description: Recover the ECDSA private key from two signatures that reuse the same nonce (k). Use the private key to derive the flag.
challenges.hint: If ECDSA uses same k for two signatures: k = (z1 - z2) / (s1 - s2) mod n, then d = r^-1(s*k - z) mod n (FREE). Private key d encodes the flag (5 pts).

challenges.title: Lattice Shatter
challenges.description: RSA with partially known plaintext. Recover the full plaintext using Coppersmith's attack.
challenges.hint: Coppersmith's attack finds small roots of polynomials (FREE). Use known flag prefix to construct polynomial (5 pts). Use LLL lattice reduction to find roots (5 pts).

challenges.title: Curve Phantom
challenges.description: Recover the private key d where Q = d*G using Pohlig-Hellman. The curve order is smooth (has small prime factors).
challenges.hint: Pohlig-Hellman: factor curve order into primes (FREE). Solve dlog separately for each prime factor (5 pts). Use CRT to combine solutions (5 pts).

challenges.title: DoubleForge
challenges.description: A known plaintext is encrypted twice with two different single-byte keys. Recover both keys using a meet-in-the-middle attack.
challenges.hint: For each possible K1: compute intermediate = Enc(P, K1) (FREE). For each possible K2: check if Dec(C, K2) matches any intermediate (5 pts). When match found: K1 and K2 recovered (5 pts).

challenges.title: EXIF Ghost
challenges.description: An image file contains hidden metadata. Analyze it to uncover the flag.
challenges.hint: Use exiftool image.jpg to view all metadata. Check the Comment field for hidden messages.

challenges.title: Shredder Recovery
challenges.description: A file was deleted from a disk image. Recover it to find the hidden flag.
challenges.hint: Use strings on the disk image to find text patterns. Look for file signatures like PNG or TXT.

challenges.title: TraceVector
challenges.description: Web server logs contain suspicious activity. Analyze them to find the encoded flag.
challenges.hint: Look for unusual User-Agent strings or suspicious query parameters. Check for base64 encoded data.

challenges.title: MagicBytes
challenges.description: A file has been renamed with the wrong extension to hide its true type. Identify the real format and extract the flag.
challenges.hint: Use: file suspicious.txt - it will show the true type. Or check the first bytes with hexdump -C.

challenges.title: Ghost Records
challenges.description: An SQLite database has deleted records. Recover them to reveal the flag.
challenges.hint: Use strings on the database file to find deleted data. Deleted records remain in unallocated pages.

challenges.title: PacketReaper
challenges.description: Network traffic was captured. Analyze the packets to extract the hidden flag.
challenges.hint: Open the PCAP in Wireshark and filter for HTTP traffic. Check HTTP headers and query parameters.

challenges.title: Sector Phantom
challenges.description: A disk image contains a hidden partition. Find it and recover the flag.
challenges.hint: Use fdisk -l to list partitions. Check for orphaned partition entries in the MBR (sector 0).

challenges.title: StegoWraith
challenges.description: A flag is hidden in an image using steganography. Extract it.
challenges.hint: Check with stegdetect first. Try zsteg for PNG LSB analysis. Try steghide with common passwords.

challenges.title: ChronoTrace
challenges.description: Multiple files with different timestamps tell a story. Reconstruct the timeline to reveal the flag.
challenges.hint: Check file modification times with stat. Look for suspicious timestamp gaps or patterns.

challenges.title: Bootkit Specter
challenges.description: A firmware image has been tampered with. Analyze it to find the hidden flag.
challenges.hint: Use binwalk to analyze the firmware structure. Run strings on extracted modules.

challenges.title: MemVault
challenges.description: A memory dump contains encrypted data and its encryption key. Find both and decrypt the flag.
challenges.hint: Use strings to find ASCII patterns. AES keys are typically 16, 24, or 32 bytes. Look for key-like byte sequences.

challenges.title: VaultCracker
challenges.description: An encrypted volume contains the flag. Identify the encryption and recover the data.
challenges.hint: TrueCrypt volumes have a specific header signature. Look for "TRUE" or "VeraCrypt" in the hex dump.

challenges.title: Operation Benazir
challenges.description: Nawabshah district was renamed to Shaheed Benazirabad in 2020. Research the citys history, landmarks, and administrative divisions to find the flag containing the districts main commodity/crop and founding information.
challenges.hint: The city is famous for **** cultivation in Sindh province. Research when the district was established. Check official government websites for district info

challenges.title: Operation Minaret
challenges.description: Hyderabad is a historic city in Sindh with the famous Minar-e-Taban and other landmarks. Find information about the citys main historical monument and the year of a significant event to extract the flag.
challenges.hint: The famous minaret/monument in Hyderabad is called Minar-e-Taban. Research when Hyderabad was elevated to city status. Check Wikipedia or Pakistan tourism websites

challenges.title: Operation Garden Vault
challenges.description: Lahore is the cultural heart of Pakistan with rich Mughal history. Research the citys famous monuments, gardens, and historical significance to find the flag containing the name of the iconic garden and the year of a major landmark.
challenges.hint: Lahore is famous for Badshahi Mosque and a historic garden nearby. Research the famous Mughal garden in Lahore city center. Check historical records for Mughal-era monuments

challenges.title: NovaSec Portal
challenges.description: NovaSec Labs just launched their shiny new company portal. Their security team is confident there's nothing interesting here ‚Äî 'just a clean page,' they said. But web servers talk in more ways than one. Sometimes the most important message isn't what gets displayed on screen.
challenges.hint: Web servers communicate using more than just HTML. Open DevTools. Click the Network tab. Reload the page. Select the main document request. Look at the Response Headers panel.

challenges.title: TimeVault
challenges.description: TimeVault is counting down to something classified. The developers were in a rush and pushed their work straight to production. While everyone stares at the ticking clock, the real secret is hidden in how the page is dressed ‚Äî not in what it does.
challenges.hint: The secret isn't in the JavaScript. Look at how the page is styled. CSS supports custom properties ‚Äî variables defined at :root. Developers sometimes store things there out of habit. Check the style tag in the page head.

challenges.title: DebugMode
challenges.description: CGS SysMonitor is an internal dashboard used to track system health across the Cyber Guardians infrastructure. An engineer left debug mode enabled before deploying to production. The logs on screen look normal. But not everything shows on screen ‚Äî and not everything the system says is for your eyes. Or is it?
challenges.hint: Developers leave messages while they code. Where do those messages usually go? Open your browser DevTools and click the Console tab. Then wait a few seconds and watch carefully.

announcements.title: CGS CTF 2026
announcements.message:
The stage has been set.
The tasks have been locked.
Now it‚Äôs time to showcase your talents.

üî• The Cyber Guardians Society (CGS) introduces CGS CTF 2026 ‚Äì the online Capture The Flag challenge for enthusiastic hackers, problem-solvers, and cybersecurity buffs who wish to be the guardians of the cyberworld.

Be a rookie in cybersecurity or an experienced competitor aiming to climb to the top of the leaderboard; this CTF challenge is meant for you.

announcements.title: CGS CTF 2026
announcements.message:
Im very happy to announce that üòã 

The Cyber Guardians Society CTF 2026 event will be officially hosted and organized on 4th August. The platform is going to be deployed soon, and participants will be able to register directly on the CTF platform instead of using Google Forms. After registration, accounts will go through admin approval before access is granted.

All participants must carefully save their login credentials during registration. You need to remember your username, which will also be your scoreboard name, along with your password. Do not rely on memory alone. Write it down somewhere safe or store it in a place you can easily access later, because these credentials will be required to log in when the event starts.

‚ùó‚ùó Important note for all users: ‚ùó‚ùó 
the login system uses a strong security mechanism called an HTTP tarpit to prevent brute-force attacks. This means if you enter incorrect credentials multiple times, each failed attempt will increase the delay before your next try. The system will slow down repeated wrong logins more and more. So make sure you log in carefully and correctly on your first few attempts to avoid delays.

Further details, rules, and updates will be shared after deployment.
